title: Fix Deprecated OpenAI Model for Formula Description Popup
description:
The GPT-powered formula description feature stopped returning results because the `text-davinci-003` model was deprecated by OpenAI. The API needed to be updated to use the `gpt-3.5-turbo` model with the chat completions endpoint.

The fix involved migrating from the legacy completions API (`/v1/completions`) to the chat completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) with the appropriate message format including a system role prompt for Salesforce expertise.
acceptance_criteria: Formula description popup returns results using the gpt-3.5-turbo model
API calls use the chat completions endpoint format
System prompt establishes Salesforce administrator expertise context
middleware_changes: Migrated OpenAI integration from text-davinci-003 completions API to gpt-3.5-turbo chat completions API. Updated request format to include system and user role messages.
api_changes: Outbound API call changed from POST /v1/completions to POST /v1/chat/completions with the gpt-3.5-turbo model and chat message format.
implementation_notes: The PHP implementation uses Laravel's Http facade. The system prompt is set to 'You are a helpful assistant' with the formula content as the user message. Temperature set to 0.5 with max_tokens of 1000.
comment_summary: Developer identified the deprecation from the OpenAI error response and found the migration guide. Updated the API and deployed to staging. Reporter then asked about explicitly moving to gpt-3.5-turbo, which had already been done.
